topic: Istanbul traffic rant — no parking anywhere

turns.content: I need to ask you something... is there a good parking app for Istanbul... because I am losing my mind

I can definitely help with that! Istanbul parking is notoriously tough. A few apps that people find useful:

- **ISPARK** — the official Istanbul municipality parking app. Shows availability in ISPARK lots and on-street spots across the city.
- **Parklio** and **Park Et** are also popular for finding nearby open spots.

Which area are you usually trying to park in? That might help me narrow down the best option.
Kadıköy... every morning the same story. I drive 5 minutes to get to work... then 25 minutes to find a place for the car. it is madness

Kadıköy is one of the worst spots in the city for parking, honestly. It's dense, lots of foot traffic, and the streets weren't designed for this many cars.

ISPARK has a few lots around Kadıköy — there's one near the ferry terminal and a couple along Bahariye. The app shows real-time availability so you can check before you leave home. Have you tried any of the side streets further from the main market area?

I have tried everything my friend... every side street, every back alley. this morning I parked 4 streets away. 4 streets... I am not a young man, walking 4 streets with my bag at 7 in the morning... and then some kid in a little Fiat squeezes into the last spot right in front of me. right in front of my eyes

That's incredibly frustrating, especially when you're on foot carrying things. The motorcycle cutting in is just salt in the wound.

A few options to consider:

1. **Monthly reserved spot** — some private garages and lots in Kadıköy offer monthly passes. More expensive than street parking but guaranteed. Could be worth the peace of mind.
2. **Arrive earlier** — I know it's not ideal, but even 20 minutes earlier can make a huge difference in Kadıköy.
3. **Park-and-ride** — some people park further out near a metro or ferry stop and take transit for the last stretch.

Would any of those work for your situation?

I already open the shop at 8... you want me there at 7:30? I would have to leave the house at 7... my wife would think I have gone crazy. and the monthly garage... I looked. they want 3,000 lira a month. 3,000 lira to put your car in a concrete box... this city has lost its mind

3,000 lira a month is steep, I agree. That's a significant monthly expense just to park.

Honest question — have you considered not driving at all? Kadıköy has great public transit connections. Depending on where you live, the dolmuş, bus, or ferry might actually be faster than driving plus the parking hunt. And you'd save on gas and parking stress.

you know... my father would laugh at this conversation. in his time there were maybe 10 cars on the whole street. you could park a truck in front of the mosque and nobody would say a word... now there are 100 cars for every 10 spots. and they keep building apartments but no parking. who is planning this city... nobody, that is who

Your father had the right era for driving in Istanbul! The city's growth has completely outpaced its infrastructure. You're right that the planning hasn't kept up — new residential buildings go up constantly, but parking infrastructure barely moves.

It's a common complaint across Istanbul. The population doubled in 25 years but the roads and parking stayed the same. Kadıköy especially — it's an old neighbourhood that was never built for this volume.

exactly... exactly. they say 'the fish rots from the head' and this is the same. the planning starts wrong, everything after is wrong. anyway... I will try this ISPARK app you said. maybe it helps, maybe not... but at least I will stop circling the streets like a lost tourist every morning

Ha — circling like a lost tourist, I like that. Give ISPARK a try, it's free to download. Check it the night before or first thing in the morning to see which lots near you have spots. Even saving 10 minutes of circling would be worth it.

And if all else fails, maybe one day Kadıköy will catch up with a proper parking plan. Until then, good luck with the morning battle!

one day... one day. inşallah before I am too old to drive. thank you my friend... I will download it tonight. if Ayşe can show me how... these phones, you know... the buttons are too small for my fingers

Ha, you'll get the hang of it. The ISPARK app is pretty straightforward — big map, colored markers for available spots. If you need help navigating it after you download it, feel free to come back and I can walk you through it. Good luck tomorrow morning!
